title: Rivian Achieves First Gross Profit in Q4 2024
text: Rivian hit a significant milestone by achieving positive gross profit in the fourth quarter. The EV manufacturer reported a gross profit of $170 million, representing a $776 million improvement compared to the same period in 2023. The company's total fourth-quarter revenue reached $1.73 billion, surpassing Wall Street expectations of $1.4 billion. Of this revenue, $1.52 billion came from automotive sales, generated from 14,183 vehicle deliveries. Additional revenue streams included $299 million from regulatory credits and $484 million from software and services. The automotive gross profit stood at $110 million for the quarter, a dramatic turnaround from a $611 million loss in Q4 2023. Full-year results showed a negative automotive gross profit of just $7 million, an improvement from the $12 million loss in 2023. CEO RJ Scaringe noted: This quarter, we achieved positive gross profit and removed $31,000 in automotive cost of goods sold per vehicle delivered in Q4 2024 relative to Q4 2023. Looking ahead, Rivian is preparing to launch its more affordable R2 electric SUV, priced around $45,000, which represents a major expansion into the mass-market segment. The company closed a $5.8 billion joint venture with Volkswagen and secured a $6.6 billion Department of Energy loan to fund manufacturing expansion in Georgia. For 2025, Rivian projects deliveries between 46,000 and 51,000 vehicles while expecting modest gross profit improvement.
